api/spec/packages/aip/src/events.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the metering event ingest and query API: CloudEvents-based MeteringEvent model, IngestedEvent with metadata, filter model, and operations for batch ingest and cursor-paginated listing. All ingest operations are fire-and-forget (202) with three @sharedRoute overloads per content-type.

patterns.name: @sharedRoute for content-type overloads
patterns.description: The ingest endpoint has three operation declarations (single CE, batch CE, JSON) all with the same @operationId and @sharedRoute so they compile to one OpenAPI operation with multiple requestBody content types.
patterns.example: @post @operationId("ingest-metering-events") @sharedRoute ingestEvent(@header contentType: "application/cloudevents+json", @body body: MeteringEvent): IngestEventsResponse | Common.ErrorResponses;

patterns.name: Cursor pagination for event listing
patterns.description: Events use ...Common.CursorPaginationQuery (not PagePaginationQuery) and return Shared.CursorPaginatedResponse<IngestedEvent>. Filter and sort params are separate named query params.
patterns.example: @get list(...Common.CursorPaginationQuery, @query filter?: ListEventsParamsFilter, @query sort?: Common.SortQuery): Shared.CursorPaginatedResponse<IngestedEvent> | Common.ErrorResponses;

patterns.name: Filter model uses Common.*FieldFilter types
patterns.description: ListEventsParamsFilter fields reference Common.StringFieldFilter, Common.ULIDFieldFilter, Common.DateTimeFieldFilter — never inline filter logic or define custom filter operators.
patterns.example: model ListEventsParamsFilter { id?: Common.StringFieldFilter; customer_id?: Common.ULIDFieldFilter; time?: Common.DateTimeFieldFilter; }

patterns.name: Ingest response is 202 Accepted with empty body
patterns.description: IngestEventsResponse declares @statusCode _: 202 and no @body, reflecting async processing semantics. Ingest is fire-and-forget, not paginated.

patterns.name: CloudEvents field constraints
patterns.description: MeteringEvent fields follow CloudEvents spec: id/source/type/subject have @minLength(1); specversion has a default of '1.0'; data is Record<unknown>.

patterns.name: deepObject filter params with explode
patterns.description: Filter parameters use @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) so filter[field][op]=value query strings deserialize correctly on the server side.

key_file_guides.role: MeteringEvent (CloudEvents schema) and IngestedEvent (with ingested_at, stored_at, validation_errors). Core model file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IngestedEventValidationError has code+message+attributes — new validation error codes added in the Go layer must have corresponding documentation here.

key_file_guides.role: EventsOperations interface with list (cursor-paginated GET) and three @sharedRoute POST overloads for ingest. Filter uses @query(#{style:"deepObject", explode:true}).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All three ingest overloads share the same @operationId. Adding a fourth content-type overload must also use @sharedRoute and the same operationId to avoid generating a separate OpenAPI operation.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel import: re-exports event.tsp and operations.tsp so callers only need import ./events/index.tsp.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new .tsp file added to this folder must be imported here.

anti_patterns: Adding pagination to the ingest endpoint — ingest is fire-and-forget 202, not paginated
Using PagePaginationQuery for event listing — events use cursor pagination (after/before), not page-number pagination
Defining filter operators inline in operations instead of using Common.*FieldFilter types
Removing @sharedRoute from any of the ingest overloads — this collapses three content-type variants to one in the OpenAPI output
Adding response body fields to IngestEventsResponse — ingest acknowledgment is intentionally empty

decisions.decision: Three @sharedRoute ingest overloads instead of a single union body
decisions.rationale: OpenAPI requires distinct requestBody entries per content-type for correct SDK code generation; TypeSpec @sharedRoute expresses this without duplicating the operation ID.
Event streams grow continuously and page-number pagination is unstable on append-only data; cursor-based (after/before) pagination gives stable traversal.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter field to event listing

api/spec/packages/aip/src/features.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Features domain: feature models (meter-backed with optional unit costs), cost query types, and CRUD+query operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI and Go/JS/Python SDKs. All types live under namespace Features; and operations are split across two interfaces (FeatureOperations and FeatureCostOperations).

patterns.name: Namespace isolation
patterns.description: All types and interfaces are declared inside namespace Features;. Every .tsp file opens with this namespace declaration to keep generated names scoped.

patterns.name: Discriminated union for polymorphic types
patterns.description: Use @discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" }) on unions (e.g. FeatureUnitCost) so the generated OpenAPI uses a type field as discriminator without extra envelope wrapping.
patterns.example:
@discriminated(#{ envelope: "none", discriminatorPropertyName: "type" })
union FeatureUnitCost { manual: FeatureManualUnitCost, llm: FeatureLlmUnitCost }

patterns.name: Visibility annotations control lifecycle access
patterns.description: Use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create) etc. on fields to control which HTTP operations expose or accept a field. Read-only computed fields use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) only.

patterns.name: Extension markers for private/unstable/internal endpoints
patterns.description: Operations not yet public must carry the appropriate extension markers. All current feature operations carry @extension(Shared.UnstableExtension, true) and @extension(Shared.InternalExtension, true).

patterns.name: Shared generic response wrappers
patterns.description: Operations return Shared.PagePaginatedResponse<T>, Shared.CreateResponse<T>, Shared.UpdateResponse<T>, Shared.DeleteResponse — never inline response shapes.

patterns.name: Suppress linter rules with inline justification
patterns.description: When a field name pattern legitimately violates the @openmeter/api-spec-aip linter (e.g. nullable fields, repeated-prefix grouping), suppress with #suppress and a quoted reason string.

key_file_guides.role: Declares FeatureOperations and FeatureCostOperations interfaces with all HTTP verbs, route params, and response types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;` — omitting this breaks @query/@get/@post decorators. deepObject+explode style required for filter params.

key_file_guides.role: Defines FeatureUnitCost discriminated union (manual/llm), enums, and resolved pricing model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Mutually exclusive fields (e.g. provider vs provider_property) are enforced by docs only — no TypeSpec constraint; add #suppress if linter flags repeated-prefix naming.

key_file_guides.role: Core Feature model using Shared.ResourceWithKey spread and optional meter + unit_cost fields.
key_file_guides.watch_for: FeatureUpdateRequest only includes updatable fields (unit_cost); do not add read-only fields like id or created_at to it.

key_file_guides.role: FeatureCostQueryRow and FeatureCostQueryResult models for the cost query endpoint; reuses Meters.MeterQueryRequest as request body.
key_file_guides.watch_for: cost field is nullable (Shared.Numeric | null) — requires #suppress on no-nullable rule. The detail field explains why cost is null.

anti_patterns: Declaring HTTP routes directly in model files — routes belong in operations.tsp only
Defining response envelope shapes inline instead of using Shared.CreateResponse<T>/PagePaginatedResponse<T>
Adding @visibility(Lifecycle.Create) to computed/server-set fields (e.g. id, created_at, status)
Omitting the appropriate extension markers on internal-only operations
Editing generated api/v3/api.gen.go instead of regenerating after TypeSpec changes

decisions.decision: LLM unit cost uses a flat model with mutually exclusive static/property fields rather than a nested union
decisions.rationale: Keeps the API surface flat and JSON-friendly; TypeSpec discriminated unions cannot enforce mutual exclusion so the constraint is documented and validated at the Go service layer.
Cost can be unresolvable when LLM pricing data is unavailable; null is the correct semantic rather than omitting the field, even though it violates the no-nullable linter rule.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new operation to FeatureOperations in operations.tsp

api/spec/packages/aip/src/governance.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the Governance domain: batch feature-access query that resolves customer identifiers to access states across multiple features, with partial-error semantics and cursor pagination. All operations are private, unstable, and internal.

patterns.name: Partial-error response model
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryResponse combines data (successful results), errors (per-identifier failures), and meta (cursor pagination) in a single response — enabling partial success without HTTP 207. Errors use a typed enum (GovernanceQueryErrorCode).

patterns.name: BaseError extension for error models
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryError and GovernanceFeatureAccessReason both extend Shared.BaseError<TCode> with a typed enum code. Use model is Shared.BaseError<EnumType> pattern for structured error bodies.
patterns.example:
model GovernanceFeatureAccessReason is Shared.BaseError<GovernanceFeatureAccessReasonCode>;
model GovernanceQueryError is Shared.BaseError<GovernanceQueryErrorCode> { customer?: string; }

patterns.name: Record<T> for dynamic feature maps
patterns.description: GovernanceQueryResult.features is Record<GovernanceFeatureAccess> keyed by feature key — allowing a variable number of features per customer without fixed schema.

patterns.name: All operations are private/unstable/internal
patterns.description: GovernanceOperations carries all three extension markers. This is the most restrictive visibility combination in the codebase.

patterns.name: Cursor pagination on a POST body query
patterns.description: The governance query operation spreads ...Common.CursorPaginationQuery as query params alongside a @body request — enabling pagination of batch query responses.

key_file_guides.role: Defines all governance domain models: GovernanceQueryRequest, GovernanceQueryResponse, GovernanceQueryResult, GovernanceFeatureAccess, reason codes, and error types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: matched field on GovernanceQueryResult is a string[] of all customer keys/subjects that resolved to the same customer — do not collapse to a single string. The Unknown enum variant on both error enums is a forward-compatibility sentinel, not a default.

key_file_guides.role: Declares GovernanceOperations interface with the single query POST endpoint.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. The operation is a POST with both spread cursor params and a @body — both are required for paginated batch queries.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel: imports governance.tsp and operations.tsp, reopens namespace Governance;
key_file_guides.watch_for: New .tsp files must be added to index.tsp.

anti_patterns: Returning HTTP 207 Multi-Status instead of the data+errors+meta partial-success envelope pattern
Using a GET with query params for the governance query — the request body can be large (up to 100 customer keys) and needs POST
Omitting any of the three extension markers (Private/Unstable/Internal) on new governance operations
Adding optional fields to GovernanceQueryRequest without specifying default values (include_credits defaults to false is the existing precedent)

decisions.decision: Single POST /query endpoint with partial-error response rather than per-customer GET endpoints
decisions.rationale: Batch evaluation of up to 100 customers across all features in one round-trip is a latency requirement; partial errors (CustomerNotFound) are expected and must not abort the entire response.

decisions.decision: Unknown sentinel value on all error code enums
decisions.rationale: Forward compatibility — new error codes added in future server versions degrade gracefully to Unknown on older clients rather than failing to deserialize.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new access denial reason to GovernanceFeatureAccessReasonCode

api/spec/packages/aip/src/invoices.purpose: TypeSpec type definitions for invoice-related models (InvoiceNumber scalar, BillingParty, tax identity, addresses) shared across billing operations in the v3 API. This folder contains only type definitions — invoice CRUD operations live in the billing/ namespace.

patterns.name: Scalar types for domain-constrained strings
patterns.description: Use scalar X extends string with @minLength/@maxLength/@example for strongly-typed domain identifiers like InvoiceNumber rather than using plain string.

patterns.name: Explicit visibility on every mutable field
patterns.description: Every field on party models explicitly declares @visibility(Lifecycle.Read, Lifecycle.Create, Lifecycle.Update) or a subset — no implicit all-lifecycle fields.

patterns.name: friendlyName for SDK type naming
patterns.description: Use @friendlyName("Billing<Name>") to control the generated SDK type name and avoid collisions with Shared namespace types.

patterns.name: Intentional omissions documented in comments
patterns.description: Fields excluded from the GOBL party spec are documented in a block comment in party.tsp with explicit rationale. Do not silently add GOBL fields.

key_file_guides.role: Defines BillingParty, BillingPartyAddresses, BillingPartyTaxIdentity — the supplier/customer party models used on invoices.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Many intentionally omitted GOBL fields are documented in a comment block (identities, people, inboxes, emails, websites, telephones, registration, logos, ext) — do not add them without design discussion.

key_file_guides.role: Defines only the InvoiceNumber scalar type. Full invoice body models live in the billing/ namespace.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add full invoice models here; they belong in the billing/ namespace. This file is a thin type definition only.
No namespace declaration in this index.tsp — callers import types by their fully qualified Invoices.* name.

anti_patterns: Adding full invoice CRUD operations here — operations belong in the billing/ folder's operations.tsp
Adding GOBL fields that are documented as intentionally omitted (identities, logos, registration, ext, etc.) without design discussion
Defining party types without @friendlyName — generates SDK names that clash with Shared namespace types
Using plain string instead of the InvoiceNumber scalar for invoice number fields

decisions.decision: BillingParty is kept intentionally minimal vs full GOBL party spec
decisions.rationale: Many GOBL fields (identity codes, people, inboxes, websites, logos) are not needed for OpenMeter's billing use case and are explicitly excluded per inline comments in party.tsp to avoid API surface bloat.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new optional address type to BillingPartyAddresses

api/spec/packages/aip/src/llmcost.purpose: TypeSpec definitions for the LLM cost pricing domain: global synced price models, per-namespace override CRUD, and filter/pagination operations compiled to v3 OpenAPI. Two separate interfaces split global read-only prices from namespace-scoped override management.

patterns.name: Split interfaces by resource boundary
patterns.description: Separate LLMCostPricesOperations (global read-only prices) from LLMCostOverridesOperations (namespace-scoped CRUD) into distinct interfaces so Go generates separate handler groups.
patterns.example:
interface LLMCostPricesOperations { list_prices(...); get_price(...); }
interface LLMCostOverridesOperations { list_overrides(...); create_override(...); delete_override(...); }

patterns.name: deepObject filter params with explode
patterns.description: All filter parameters use @query(#{ style: "deepObject", explode: true }) so filter[field][op]=value query strings deserialize correctly.

patterns.name: Read-only visibility for all Price response fields
patterns.description: All Price model fields use @visibility(Lifecycle.Read) since prices are system-managed. OverrideCreate is a separate flat input model for writes — never mix read and write fields on the same model.

patterns.name: Enum for controlled string values
patterns.description: Use enum (not union) for closed string sets with known members like PriceSource. Use union only when open-ended or when needing @summary per variant.

patterns.name: Suppress repeated-prefix linter with justification
patterns.description: Fields like model_id/model_name, cache_read_per_token/cache_write_per_token, and effective_from/effective_to trigger the repeated-prefix-grouping linter; suppress with a specific reason string.
patterns.example: #suppress "@openmeter/api-spec-aip/repeated-prefix-grouping" "model_id and model_name should not be grouped"

key_file_guides.role: Declares LLMCostPricesOperations and LLMCostOverridesOperations interfaces with all HTTP endpoints and ListPricesParamsFilter filter model.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must import @typespec/http and use `using TypeSpec.Http;`. Sort parameter is documented on list_prices but not list_overrides — keep consistent when extending. All operations require UnstableExtension and InternalExtension markers.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Price, ModelPricing, Provider, Model, OverrideCreate models and PriceSource enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: OverrideCreate uses model_id (plain string) not a ULID — override creation is upsert semantics (unique per provider+model+currency), not idempotent by ID. effective_from is required on OverrideCreate.

key_file_guides.role: Barrel: imports prices.tsp then operations.tsp in that order (models before operations).
key_file_guides.watch_for: Order matters — operations.tsp imports types from prices.tsp; the index must maintain this import order.

anti_patterns: Merging global price operations and override operations into a single interface
Using @visibility(Lifecycle.Create/Update) on Price model fields — prices are read-only responses; OverrideCreate is the separate write input model
Omitting effective_from on OverrideCreate — it is required to establish when the override takes effect
Using a ULID for OverrideCreate identification — overrides are upserted by provider+model+currency composite key, not by ID

decisions.decision: OverrideCreate is a flat input model separate from the Price response model
decisions.rationale: The write input (provider string, model_id, pricing) is structurally different from the full Price response which includes system-managed fields (id, source, created_at). Sharing one model would require complex visibility gymnastics.

decisions.decision: Two interfaces (Prices vs Overrides) rather than one combined interface
decisions.rationale: Global prices are read-only and system-synced; per-namespace overrides are user-managed CRUD. Keeping them separate makes handler responsibility boundaries clear and allows independent authorization policies.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new filter field to ListPricesParamsFilter
